name: Beef En Daube
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut meat into 1-inch cubes.
2. Fry bacon until crisp.
3. Remove the bacon from the pan and discard it.
4. Pour off all but 2 tablespoons of the drippings in the pan.
5. Brown the meat and onions in the remaining drippings.
6. Transfer the browned mixture to a crockery pot.
7. Add vinegar, brown sugar, wine, salt, pepper, garlic, thyme (fresh or dried), beef stock base, and orange peel to the pot.
8. Stir until well combined.
9. Cover and cook on LOW for 8 hours.
10. After 8 hours, increase the heat to HIGH.
11. Mix cornstarch with 1 tablespoon cold water to form a slurry.
12. When the juices are bubbling, stir in the cornstarch slurry into the pot.
13. Continue cooking and stirring until the sauce thickens.
14. Garnish with chopped parsley before serving.
Makes 6 servings.
